message:
fuzz: sync Cargo.lock with recently-landed fuzz harness and crypto dep changes

Pure lockfile-resolution sync. No Cargo.toml edits in this commit;
this just catches `fuzz/Cargo.lock` up to what the resolver
computes given the workspace's current set of `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
manifests.

Four new transitive-dep links appear:

* `+sha2, +tempfile` under one of the fuzz crates -- pulled in by
  `fuzz: add iblt_decode and store_wal_recovery targets`
  (99245e69) which noted "tempfile added to fuzz dependencies for
  the WAL target." The resolver was not re-run at that commit.

* `+subtle` under another fuzz crate -- pulled in transitively by
  `fcp-crypto: use ed25519 verify_strict to close signature
  malleability` (1fba4a38) and by the host-side subtle add noted
  in `build(deps): sync Cargo.lock for subtle crate added to
  fcp-host` (b8c1a599, which only synced the workspace
  `Cargo.lock`, not the fuzz lockfile).

* `+ciborium` under a fuzz crate -- pulled in by `fcp-mesh:
  replace IbltPlaceholder JSON change log with production CBOR
  Iblt on gossip wire` (dfe0354a) and further exercised by
  `fuzz: add iblt_decode and store_wal_recovery targets`
  (99245e69), both of which deserialize with `ciborium`.

Why these are harmless
----------------------
`fuzz/Cargo.lock` only affects reproducibility of fuzz harness
builds. No runtime code path, no binary artifact shipped to users.
Keeping it in sync prevents the next `cargo +nightly build` in
`fuzz/` from silently re-resolving and landing a noisy
unrelated-looking diff on top of someone else's change.

Verification
------------
Confirmed each added dep has a corresponding upstream manifest or
transitive pull by walking the dependency list in the diff. No new
top-level dep added here; only `[dependencies]` links appear that
already correspond to `fuzz/*/Cargo.toml` additions in earlier
commits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
